html:
Daily Economic Briefing

Friday, 10 July 2026

MOVE 72.41 Elevated SOFR 3.58% Stable NFCI −0.515 Loose WASDE 12:00 ET
📊 Liquidity & Stress Metrics — The "Gnosis" Pulse
Current regime: Structural Expansion with an energy-led inflation and rate-volatility overlay. Secured funding is calm and broad financial conditions remain loose, but Treasury volatility is elevated enough for oil or geopolitical headlines to transmit quickly into yields and ES.
MetricValueMarket ContextStatus
MOVE Index72.41Latest delayed close is 08 July. Treasury volatility has repriced higher, keeping term-premium and oil-inflation shocks relevant even without a Treasury auction today.Elevated
SOFR Rate3.58%New York Fed effective rate for 08 July, down from 3.62% on 07 July. Secured overnight funding is orderly; current stress is not coming from market plumbing.Stable
NFCI−0.515Chicago Fed reading for the week ending 03 July. A negative value means broad credit, leverage, and risk conditions remain looser than their historical average.Loose
Gnosis Interpretation
The liquidity floor remains supportive: SOFR eased to 3.58% and NFCI at −0.515 still signals loose financial conditions. MOVE at 72.41 is the constraint. With no Treasury supply or top-tier U.S. macro release, the key question is whether energy headlines and today's supply-demand reports lift inflation expectations enough to keep rates volatility elevated.
📅 Economic Calendar: 2026-07-10
Investing.com's U.S. two-/three-star scan was used for discovery and cross-checking; selected times were verified with IEA, USDA, New York Fed, Baker Hughes, CFTC, and Federal Reserve sources. No BLS, Census, or BEA high-impact release, Treasury auction, scheduled Fed speaker, or FOMC event is due today.
EventET (US)GMT+8 (Local)ImportanceNotes
IEA Oil Market Report, July04:0016:00Medium-HighReleased at 10:00 Paris time. Supply, demand, inventory, and refining revisions matter unusually much while U.S.-Iran tensions keep the Strait of Hormuz risk premium unstable.
USDA WASDE & Crop Production12:0000:00 (+1)Medium-HighPrimary USDA schedules confirm both reports. Grain, oilseed, and food-supply surprises can move agricultural futures and the food-inflation narrative.
New York Fed Staff Nowcast12:4500:45 (+1)MediumFriday growth-tracking update using data available through 10:00 ET. More useful for the growth mix than as a first-order ES catalyst.
Baker Hughes U.S. Rig Count13:0001:00 (+1)MediumWeekly U.S. oil and total rig activity. Watch whether domestic supply response is improving as geopolitical risk keeps crude sensitive.
CFTC Commitments of Traders15:3003:30 (+1)MediumOfficial July schedule confirms the release. Tuesday positioning across equity index, rates, energy, and commodity futures is a post-session market-structure check.
Federal Reserve H.8 Bank Balance Sheets16:1504:15 (+1)MediumWeekly aggregate commercial-bank balance sheet. Track deposits, bank credit, and lending for confirmation that loose broad conditions are still reaching the real economy.
🏛️ Geopolitical & Fed Watch
U.S.-Iran Truce Risk & Oil Transmission
  • Headline Risk: Fresh strikes and uncertain shipping through the Strait of Hormuz keep oil capable of repricing inflation expectations before scheduled U.S. data arrives.
  • Cross-Asset Lens: Crude up with Treasury yields and MOVE is the hostile ES mix. Oil easing while SOFR stays calm restores the liquidity-led risk-on setup.
Food & Energy Supply Pulse
  • Morning: IEA revisions frame the global petroleum balance after recent conflict-driven inventory stress.
  • Midday: WASDE, Crop Production, and the rig count form a compact supply-side inflation sequence from agriculture to domestic energy capacity.
Fed & Liquidity Check
  • Policy Calendar: No scheduled Fed speaker or FOMC decision/minutes event today. The next major policy-risk window is next week's inflation sequence and Beige Book.
  • Plumbing: SOFR and NFCI remain benign; the 16:15 ET H.8 release is the late confirmation check on deposits, credit creation, and bank balance sheets.
📈 Trading Strategy Note (ES Futures)
The Breakout Blueprint: Keep the structural long bias while SOFR and NFCI stay supportive, but require crude and Treasury volatility to cooperate. MOVE above 72 makes a headline-driven yield spike more dangerous than the light macro calendar suggests.
Target Horizons: The clean continuation setup is oil contained, long yields stable, and ES holding value after the 12:00–13:00 ET supply-data cluster. If oil and yields rise together, reduce target ambition and favor mean-reversion exits.
Order-Flow Execution: Monitor 1.5k MotiveWave volume charts around 12:00 ET WASDE/Crop Production, 12:45 ET Nowcast, and 13:00 ET rig count. Avoid first-touch entries during commodity-led yield spikes; wait for absorption and cross-asset confirmation.
The Session Shape: Light top-tier macro favors range trade and headline sensitivity before noon, then a clustered supply-data window. This is a regular Friday expiration, not monthly or quarterly options expiry; CFTC and H.8 arrive after the main cash-session decision window.
